subagent_reports.topic: Discovery opportunity scout — design quality gates

sources.label: subagent-reports.ts DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema
sources.summary: design_dimensions (incl. site_design_consistency) is typed+strict but has no orchestration/review/gate consumer.

sources.label: design_dimensions consumer search
sources.summary: Only schema, prompt (adv-designer.md), specs, and asset tests reference it — confirmed sidecar with no fate.

sources.label: evidence-policy.ts
sources.summary: design_proof evidence policy already exists in ContractEvidencePolicy vocabulary; not yet mapped to designer dimensions.

sources.label: required_follow_ups obligation_class path
sources.summary: required_follow_ups with obligation_class=required_critical already auto-creates priority-tagged agenda items — reusable rail for designer concerns.

sources.label: VIEWPORT/screenshot baseline search
sources.locator: lgrep VIEWPORT viewport screenshot baseline across /home/jon/dev/advance
sources.summary: Zero matches — no viewport-target or screenshot-baseline anchor exists in any packet/schema.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Discovery already names the 5 obvious directions (frontend metadata validator, visual context packet, designer concern handling, acceptance proof mapping, browser evidence). The under-specified leverage gap is the consumer wiring: design_dimensions — especially site_design_consistency, the exact page-fit signal motivating this change — is a typed strict sidecar with no contract/gate/agenda consumer. Two existing rails (design_proof evidence policy in evidence-policy.ts, and required_follow_ups obligation_class agenda promotion) already exist and can be reused instead of building net-new gate machinery, reducing risk and respecting the 'structural correctness first / preserve 7 gates' constraints.

validation.notes: All candidates tie to a proposal contract item and have file/search evidence. No constraint/avoidance conflicts; no scope expansion beyond stated objectives.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the consumer-wiring framing as the discovery spine: map design_dimensions (concern/fail) to design_proof review-matrix rows for UI changes, and ride concern surfacing on the existing required_follow_ups obligation_class rail rather than new gate code. Surface to user the two genuine policy tradeoffs (six-dimension rigor tier; whether neighboring concerns block acceptance vs HITL) since they are user-value decisions, not auto-adoptable.
follow_ups: Confirm during design whether design_proof rows are minted at contract-mint time (prep) or appended at review time.
Decide rigor tiering: all-six-dimensions vs documented smaller rubric for low-risk UI tasks (user-surface).

subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage scout — design quality gates

sources.label: task-classifier.ts (rq-TDD004cls)
sources.summary: Metadata-first authoritative + title-heuristic warn-only fallback is the proven, shipped template for the draft's frontend classification. Reuse this exact shape; do not invent a parallel classifier.

sources.label: checkNonCodeEvidencePolicy (rq-nonCodeWorkflow01)
sources.summary: Existing structural prep-readiness check keyed on task metadata that errors when a required policy field is missing and demands a bounded rationale for the 'opt-out' value. Exact template for the metadata.frontend / frontend_rationale gate.

sources.label: DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema
sources.summary: Six dimensions already typed strict, plus an optional singular 'notes' field. design_dimensions/neighboring_recommendations are 'worker_derived' but have ZERO runtime consumers (grep: only schema/prompt/asset-tests reference them).

sources.label: evidence-policy.ts
sources.summary: design_proof and rubric_review evidence policies already in the shared vocabulary used by contract items + review-matrix rows. No new evidence vocab needed.

sources.label: acceptance review-matrix gate
sources.summary: Acceptance blocks when contract.reviewMatrix missing OR any verificationRequired!==false item lacks a matrix row (ACCEPTANCE_REVIEW_ROW_MISSING). This is the durable rail concern→proof must ride; mint design_proof rows so concerns become matrix evidence.

sources.label: Playwright MCP already granted
sources.summary: playwright_* tool grant + skill("playwright-mcp") already wired into BOTH designer and reviewer packets. Browser-evidence mechanism exists; draft only makes its use a structural expectation, satisfying the no-Storybook avoidance.

sources.label: designer report scope = task
sources.summary: adv-designer report is TaskScoped (has task_id) → persists on task.subagent_reports[], not change sidecar. Concern consumer in adv-review must read task.subagent_reports[], matching change.ts:2557.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment:
The draft design is structurally sound and already maps onto four shipped rails — no net-new gate machinery required, satisfying the 'preserve seven gates / structural-correctness-first' constraints. Five leverage/simplification candidates, ranked by payoff:

[C1 — HIGH payoff / LOW risk] Frontend classifier: model the prep-readiness frontend check directly on task-classifier.ts (metadata-first authoritative, title heuristics warn-only) and on checkNonCodeEvidencePolicy (error when required field missing, bounded rationale for opt-out). This is a near-mechanical clone of two proven patterns and directly discharges the 'not heuristic-only' constraint structurally. Adoption: design references both modules as templates; implementation lands a checkFrontendApplicability() beside checkNonCodeEvidencePolicy.

[C2 — HIGH payoff / LOW risk] Consumer wiring is THE leverage point: design_dimensions is a typed strict sidecar with zero consumers today. The whole user value of this change is wiring concern/n_a verdicts into the existing design_proof/rubric_review review-matrix rows so ACCEPTANCE_REVIEW_ROW_MISSING already enforces them. No new blocker code — reuse gate-readiness.ts:413-429. Adoption: design pins where rows are minted (prep at contract-mint vs review-time append — see follow-up).

[C3 — MEDIUM payoff / LOW risk SIMPLIFICATION] The draft's 'superRefine requiring rationale when concern|n/a' duplicates intent the schema already half-supports via the optional 'notes' field. Simpler path: tighten the EXISTING DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema with a superRefine that requires notes (or per-dimension note) when any dimension is concern|n/a, rather than adding parallel dimension_notes/rationales objects. Fewer new fields, preserves pass|concern|n/a compatibility, less asset-test churn. Risk: a per-dimension note map is richer than a single notes string — confirm whether one shared rationale suffices for the rubric.

[C4 — MEDIUM payoff / LOW risk] Neighboring recommendations: DesignerNeighboringRecommendationSchema already exists and is worker_derived with no consumer. Draft's 'don't block checkpoint; surface at review/acceptance' is correct and cheap — route it through the same change/task report read in adv-review (change.ts:2557) as an include/defer/accept-debt summary item, not a new gate. Avoids over-blocking.

[C5 — MEDIUM payoff / LOW risk] Browser evidence: mechanism (playwright_* + skill) is already granted to both packets. The only structural addition is making a design_proof matrix row's evidence expectation conditional on agreement visual_surface=true with an explicit durable fallback string when no runnable surface — reusing evidence-policy + review-matrix, not new tooling. Strongly satisfies the no-Storybook avoidance.

No MAJOR deviation from canonical ADV patterns; the design is by-the-book reuse. The one genuine simplification (C3) reduces new schema surface.

validation.notes: All five candidates tie to an agreement objective/AC and have file-cited local evidence. No constraint/avoidance conflicts: seven gates preserved, adv-designer stays apply-only (task-scoped report), adv-reviewer keeps review/harden, frontend routing is structural-not-heuristic-only, browser evidence conditional on runnable surface. C3 is a simplification within scope, not new user-facing AC.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the four-rails framing but with two refinements: (1) Make C2 (consumer wiring of design_dimensions → design_proof review-matrix rows) the explicit spine of the design, since it is the actual user value and all other rails feed it. (2) Prefer C3: extend the EXISTING DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema via superRefine (require a rationale/notes when any dimension is concern|n/a) instead of adding parallel dimension_notes/rationales objects — this is the simpler equivalent and minimizes schema + asset-test churn. Surface to user only the genuine policy tradeoff already flagged in discovery (whether one shared notes string vs per-dimension rationale map). C1/C4/C5 are mechanical reuse of shipped patterns and need no user decision.
follow_ups: Design must decide where design_proof review-matrix rows for UI tasks are minted: at contract-mint (prep) so prep-readiness can pre-seed, vs appended at review-time by adv-review when designer report lands. gate-readiness.ts:413 keys off contract.items, so prep-time minting is likely cleaner.
Confirm with user/design whether a single shared notes string (current schema) suffices for concern/n/a rationale or whether per-dimension rationale map is required (C3 decision).
Verify adv-review reads task.subagent_reports[] (not just change sidecar) for designer concerns, since adv-designer reports are task-scoped (subagent-reports.ts:179).

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for design quality gates (reuse ADV lifecycle rails, no new gate)

sources.label: ADV evidence-policy schema (design_proof + rubric_review already exist)
sources.summary: D1 confirmed: design_proof and rubric_review are existing ContractEvidencePolicy enum members; reuse is real, not net-new.

sources.label: DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema (six dimensions, notes optional today)
sources.summary: Six dimension fields exist with pass/concern/n-a enum; notes is currently .optional(). D4 requires notes when any dimension is concern/n-a — a structural schema tightening, fully feasible.

sources.label: Existing remediation policy forbids accepted-debt terminal state
sources.summary: adv-review.md/adv-harden.md tests assert NO 'accepted_debt'/'accepted debt' path; terminal states are fixed | rejected_with_evidence; 'No future-work deferral for validated in-scope findings'. Design D5 / agreement AC4-AC5 use 'accepted debt' disposition — vocabulary tension.

sources.label: Scope-discovery split = legitimate defer-to-child rail
sources.summary: Existing 'split' creates a fast-follow child change for out-of-scope work. Neighboring 'defer' should map onto this rail rather than introduce a new accepted_debt terminal state.

sources.label: Existing Preview URL Proof + visual_surface acceptance path
sources.summary: live/not_applicable/blocked Preview URL proof with reachability evidence + reviewMatrix already gates acceptance for visual surfaces. D6 conditional browser evidence reuses this existing rail; no Storybook needed.

sources.label: UX critique best practice: evidence over preference
sources.summary: Canonical: ban 'I like/I don't like'; anchor all feedback to user/goals/usability/research. Supports pass/concern/n-a + required rationale + design_proof evidence over subjective scoring.

sources.label: Design Council Principles & Practice (objective, 3-state verdict)
sources.summary: Canonical 3-state verdict: support-as-stands / support-with-improvements / fundamental-rethink maps to pass/concern/blocking. Objective criteria over stylistic taste = AC8 alignment.

sources.label: Design review rubric pitfalls (score as leading indicator, not authority)
sources.summary: Numeric rubric = leading indicator, NOT causation; require written rationale to avoid score-compression. Supports avoidance: no numeric aesthetic score as sole authority.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design reuses existing ADV rails (evidence policies design_proof/rubric_review, six-dimension DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema, contract review matrix, Preview URL proof, prep-readiness validator, apply-context packets, adv-designer apply-only / adv-reviewer review-harden boundary). No new gate, no new role, no Storybook — this is the boring, proven path and is strongly endorsed by external design-review best practice (evidence over preference; 3-state verdict; numeric score never sole authority). Most decisions are schema/validator tightenings of surfaces that already exist (D1, D3, D4 confirmed in code). The single material gap is disposition-vocabulary alignment: D5/AC4/AC5 introduce 'accepted debt' for unresolved design concerns and neighboring recommendations, but the existing enforced policy (adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts + adv-review.md) explicitly forbids an 'accepted_debt' terminal state for validated in-scope findings and recognizes only fixed | rejected_with_evidence, with out-of-scope work routed via scope-discovery 'split' (fast-follow child).

validation.notes: CAUTION (not CONFLICT): design is architecturally sound and best-practice-aligned. One terminology/policy-mapping caution must be resolved during implementation to avoid contradicting enforced specs: 'accepted debt' for design concerns / neighboring recs must NOT become a new accepted_debt terminal state for validated in-scope findings. Map it onto existing vocabulary: (a) in-scope unresolved design concern => fixed | rejected_with_evidence (matching adv-review.md:492-497); (b) neighboring/out-of-scope 'defer' => scope-discovery 'split' fast-follow child (scope-discovery-protocol.md:40), surfaced before acceptance, not silently dropped. This preserves AC4/AC5 intent (surface, never silently fix/hide) without weakening the no-future-work-deferral guarantee.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning with one binding refinement: reconcile the 'accepted debt' disposition language in D5/AC4/AC5 with the existing enforced no-accepted-debt policy before writing tests. Concretely — (1) tighten DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema so notes is required when any dimension is concern or n/a (D4 needs a refine()/superRefine, since notes is .optional() today at subagent-reports.ts:166); (2) classify unresolved in-scope design concerns as fixed | rejected_with_evidence (reuse adv-review.md terminal states), and route neighboring/out-of-scope items through scope-discovery split rather than a new accepted_debt state, so the adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts assertions are not violated; (3) reuse the existing Preview URL Proof live/not_applicable/blocked path for D6 conditional browser evidence; (4) keep design-derived criterion 'hard failures structured not heuristic-only' as a schema/validator test, consistent with the warn-only heuristic stance in D2. With these mappings the design is VALIDATED-on-implementation; the only risk is shipping 'accepted_debt' literal vocabulary that collides with existing spec tests.
follow_ups: Implementation: add refine() to DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema requiring notes when any dimension is concern/n-a (notes currently .optional() at subagent-reports.ts:166).

Implementation: confirm prep-readiness frontend-applicability validation (D2) emits warn-only for heuristics and error/structural only when backed by agreement frontend scope or task metadata.frontend with frontend_rationale — no existing frontend validator found in plugin/src/validator (net-new surface).

Spec alignment: ensure new review/acceptance consumer language for design concerns/neighboring recs does not introduce 'accepted_debt' literal forbidden by adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts:79; map to fixed|rejected_with_evidence + scope-discovery split.
Note: adv_change_show/adv_spec/project_context returned no reachable workflow/specs/project.md; validation relied on inline packet + direct codebase evidence under /home/jon/dev/advance.

nonblocking_findings.why: Matches contract intent that heuristics must not solely own gate-blocking correctness.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: adv-designer design_dimensions schema requires notes when any dimension is concern or n/a.
nonblocking_findings.why: Provides structural validation before persistence for non-pass and non-applicable design evidence.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Acceptance review instructions consume adv-designer evidence into review matrix proof and block unresolved owned-scope concerns.
nonblocking_findings.why: Keeps review/harden ownership with adv-reviewer while preserving adv-designer as apply-phase evidence producer.

reentry_history.reason: Acceptance review found the change is ~60% prompt/spec prose with weak structural backing. The headline success criterion (SC2: design concerns visible before acceptance, not buried in sidecar reports) has no consumer code — design_dimensions/neighboring_recommendations have zero structural fate. Contract AC4/AC5 also still contain accept-debt language contradicting the approved design refinement. Redefining from the top to make structural enforcement the core deliverable and re-mint a self-consistent contract.
reentry_history.scope_delta: Shift from prompt-additions to structural enforcement: (1) build a real consumer that ingests persisted adv-designer design_dimensions concerns + neighboring_recommendations into the contract review matrix and/or the required_follow_ups→agenda rail at acceptance time; (2) add required_follow_ups to the designer report schema so design concerns can ride the existing obligation_class promotion; (3) keep prep-readiness + dimension-notes schema teeth; (4) re-mint contract AC to remove accept-debt vocabulary and add an explicit acceptance criterion that designer concerns are structurally surfaced/blocking, not reviewer-goodwill-dependent; (5) reframe asset tests so prose presence is not mistaken for behavioral coverage.

items.text: ADV UI work reliably gets page/site design context before implementation.

Design proof uses typed, durable ADV evidence and a structural consumer instead of prompt-only judgment.

On `adv-designer` report submission, each `neighboring_recommendation` is structurally promoted into a durable obligation; none can be silently dropped.
Unresolved promoted design concerns block the acceptance gate through existing review-matrix/obligation enforcement — no new lifecycle gate is added.
Acceptance proof includes design-quality evidence through existing contract/review evidence paths.
Runnable visual surfaces require browser/design evidence with viewport context; missing runnable surface requires explicit durable fallback rationale.
Disposition vocabulary is `fixed`, `rejected_with_evidence`, split/fast-follow, or blocking — no `accepted_debt` terminal state exists anywhere in specs, contract, commands, or agents.
The change does not add Storybook as an ADV plugin dependency, make numeric aesthetic scores sole authority, weaken backend boundaries, or route review/harden to `adv-designer`.
Asset tests that assert markdown keyword presence are explicitly labeled non-behavioral; structural behavior (schema, validator, consumer, acceptance blocking) is covered by behavioral tests.
Preserve ADV's seven-gate lifecycle and existing human checkpoints.
Structural correctness first: schemas, validators, typed reports, the consumer, review-matrix rows, and tests own correctness.
Keep `adv-designer` apply-phase only.
Keep `adv-reviewer` as review/harden owner.
Any project-specific visual identity source must be discoverable and precedence-defined.
Frontend routing correctness must not rely on filename/title heuristics as the sole authority.
Browser/design evidence is required only when a runnable UI or preview surface exists; fallback rationale is required when it does not.
Reuse existing obligation_class → agenda and review-matrix rails; do not invent a new gate or a new evidence vocabulary.
Do not weaken the existing backend boundary for `adv-designer`.
Do not silently broaden UI scope to redesign neighboring surfaces without user-visible decision handling.

Do not add Storybook as a dependency to the Advance plugin itself.
Do not reassign review or harden ownership from `adv-reviewer` to `adv-designer`.
Do not implement a full design-system product inside ADV.
Do not make numeric aesthetic scores the only acceptance or release authority.
Do not let prose or markdown-presence tests substitute for structural enforcement.

documents.proposal:
# Proposal: Structural Design-Quality Enforcement (redefined)

## Problem

ADV's design-quality work to date is mostly prompt prose. The typed `adv-designer` evidence (`design_dimensions`, `neighboring_recommendations`) has no consumer: a `concern` verdict — including `site_design_consistency`, the exact page-fit signal that motivated this change — has zero structural fate. Acceptance is "protected" only by instructions in `adv-review.md` that ask the reviewer LLM to look, plus asset tests that grep markdown for keywords. A green suite proves the words exist, not that behavior improves.

## Why redefine now

Re-entered from the top after the acceptance review found ~60% of the change was prose with weak teeth. SC2 ("design concerns visible before acceptance, not buried in sidecar reports") had no backing code. The contract itself drifted (AC4/AC5 still say "accepted debt").

## Approach — structure owns correctness (P33)

1. **Designer schema gets a promotion rail.** Add `required_follow_ups` to `DesignerSubagentReportSchema` so design concerns can ride the EXISTING `obligation_class` → agenda consumer (`consumeRequiredFollowUps` in `tools/subagent-report.ts`).
2. **Build the consumer.** On `adv_subagent_report_submit` of an `adv-designer` report, structurally promote each `design_dimensions == "concern"` and each `neighboring_recommendation` into durable fate (required-obligation agenda items and/or review-matrix obligations). Silent drop becomes impossible by code, not by prose.
3. **Acceptance blocks structurally.** Unresolved design concerns surface as failing/open obligations that the acceptance gate already enforces via the review matrix — no new gate, no prose dependency.
4. **Keep the genuine teeth.** Retain the dimension-notes `superRefine` and the prep-readiness frontend-applicability validator (these are already real).
5. **Re-mint a self-consistent contract.** Remove all accept-debt vocabulary; add an explicit acceptance criterion that designer concerns are structurally promoted and acceptance-blocking.
6. **Reframe tests.** Keep markdown-presence asset tests but add behavioral tests on the consumer, and explicitly mark presence-tests as non-behavioral so a green suite is not mistaken for outcome coverage.

## Scope boundaries (unchanged)

- No new lifecycle gate. No Storybook dependency. No numeric aesthetic scoring authority.
- `adv-designer` stays apply-phase only; `adv-reviewer` keeps review/harden ownership.
- Heuristics (filename/title) remain advisory; structured metadata/typed reports own correctness.

## Out of scope

- Full design-system product, visual-regression baselines, vendoring external design tools.
documents.problemStatement:
ADV has design-review prose and a dedicated `adv-designer`, but design quality is not structurally enforced. A first implementation pass demonstrated the failure mode directly: most of the work landed as command/agent/spec prose whose only tests assert that keywords exist in markdown files — proving the words are present, not that outcomes improve.

Concretely:
- `adv-designer` `design_dimensions` (incl. `site_design_consistency`) and `neighboring_recommendations` are typed but have NO consumer. No code surfaces a `concern` into the review matrix, the agenda, or any acceptance-blocking signal. A design concern has zero structural fate — it sits in a sidecar report and dies there.
- Frontend visual-context/routing packets are markdown templates with no code that populates or routes them.
- The minted contract permitted "accepted debt" disposition, contradicting the later design decision that forbids an accepted_debt terminal state — internal contract drift.

Desired outcome: frontend tasks reliably get visual context and routing, and unresolved design-fit concerns are promoted into durable ADV state (review matrix and/or the existing required_follow_ups→agenda obligation rail) and block acceptance by CODE — not by reviewer goodwill. Prose may guide discovery; structure must own correctness, acceptance, and gate completion (P33).
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Make frontend/UI task classification explicit and structurally checked before planning completes.
2. Provide designer workers with page/site visual context and explicit missing-context fallbacks.
3. Structurally promote designer six-dimension concerns and neighboring recommendations into durable ADV obligations consumed at acceptance — by code, not by prose.
4. Require durable design/browser evidence when a runnable visual surface exists, with explicit fallback when absent.
5. Preserve current role boundaries and avoid subjective scoring or broad redesign as hidden gate authority.

## Success Criteria

1. ADV UI work reliably gets page/site design context before implementation.
2. Frontend design concerns are promoted into durable ADV state and made acceptance-blocking by code — not buried in sidecar reports or dependent on reviewer goodwill.
3. Design proof uses typed, durable ADV evidence and a structural consumer instead of prompt-only judgment.
4. Existing role boundaries stay intact: `adv-designer` apply-only; `adv-reviewer` owns review/harden.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Frontend/UI task planning records explicit frontend applicability and routing intent, or a structured non-frontend rationale, enforced by prep-readiness validation (heuristics advisory only).
2. Frontend worker context includes visual-fit inputs: surface type, relevant existing UI patterns, constraints/avoidances, viewport/evidence expectations, or explicit unavailable markers.
3. Designer six-dimension verdicts require rationale notes for any `concern` or `n/a`, enforced structurally by the designer report schema.
4. On `adv-designer` report submission, each `design_dimensions == "concern"` is structurally promoted into a durable required obligation (the existing required_follow_ups → agenda obligation rail and/or a review-matrix obligation) — not left only in the report.
5. On `adv-designer` report submission, each `neighboring_recommendation` is structurally promoted into a durable obligation; none can be silently dropped.
6. Unresolved promoted design concerns block the acceptance gate through existing review-matrix/obligation enforcement — no new lifecycle gate is added.
7. Acceptance proof includes design-quality evidence through existing contract/review evidence paths.
8. Runnable visual surfaces require browser/design evidence with viewport context; missing runnable surface requires explicit durable fallback rationale.
9. Disposition vocabulary is `fixed`, `rejected_with_evidence`, split/fast-follow, or blocking — no `accepted_debt` terminal state exists anywhere in specs, contract, commands, or agents.
10. The change does not add Storybook as an ADV plugin dependency, make numeric aesthetic scores sole authority, weaken backend boundaries, or route review/harden to `adv-designer`.
11. Asset tests that assert markdown keyword presence are explicitly labeled non-behavioral; structural behavior (schema, validator, consumer, acceptance blocking) is covered by behavioral tests.

## Constraints

1. Preserve ADV's seven-gate lifecycle and existing human checkpoints.
2. Structural correctness first: schemas, validators, typed reports, the consumer, review-matrix rows, and tests own correctness.
3. Keep `adv-designer` apply-phase only.
4. Keep `adv-reviewer` as review/harden owner.
5. Any project-specific visual identity source must be discoverable and precedence-defined.
6. Frontend routing correctness must not rely on filename/title heuristics as the sole authority.
7. Browser/design evidence is required only when a runnable UI or preview surface exists; fallback rationale is required when it does not.
8. Reuse existing obligation_class → agenda and review-matrix rails; do not invent a new gate or a new evidence vocabulary.

## Avoidances

1. Do not weaken the existing backend boundary for `adv-designer`.
2. Do not silently broaden UI scope to redesign neighboring surfaces without user-visible decision handling.
3. Do not treat subjective visual scoring as a replacement for typed evidence, review findings, and user acceptance.
4. Do not add Storybook as a dependency to the Advance plugin itself.
5. Do not reassign review or harden ownership from `adv-reviewer` to `adv-designer`.
6. Do not implement a full design-system product inside ADV.
7. Do not make numeric aesthetic scores the only acceptance or release authority.
8. Do not let prose or markdown-presence tests substitute for structural enforcement.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: this change modifies ADV workflow, agent packets, schemas, validators, a report consumer, specs, and tests. It does not itself deliver a browser-visible UI surface. Acceptance uses tests/static checks/review evidence rather than a preview URL.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. Re-entered from the top after acceptance review found prose-heavy implementation with weak structural backing; structural enforcement is now the core deliverable.
2. Design rigor: risk-tiered. All six dimensions checked for frontend work; low-risk tasks may mark dimensions `n/a` with rationale; unresolved concerns require resolution, `rejected_with_evidence`, split/fast-follow, or blocking proof.
3. Neighboring UI issues: structurally promoted before acceptance; workers must not silently broaden scope.
4. Cross-project origin: toolbox-originated follow-up belongs in `/home/jon/dev/advance`.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. Reuse the existing `required_follow_ups` → agenda obligation rail (`consumeRequiredFollowUps`) as the promotion mechanism for design concerns.
2. Reuse existing review-matrix and acceptance-gate enforcement rather than adding a new lifecycle gate.
3. Treat `DESIGNER_REPORT.design_dimensions` and `neighboring_recommendations` as the typed source the consumer ingests.
4. Keep prep-readiness validation as the structural chokepoint for planned frontend applicability.
5. Mark markdown-presence asset tests as non-behavioral; cover the consumer with behavioral tests.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Pending user approval of re-minted criteria.
documents.design:
# Design: Structural Design-Quality Enforcement (validator-corrected)

## Architecture context (verified)

- Submit flow `tools/subagent-report.ts::executeSubmit`: parse → persist via `subagentReportSubmittedSignal` (reports land in `ChangeWorkflowState.subagent_reports` / task-scoped) → `consumeFollowUps` → `consumeRequiredFollowUps` (`category:"required-obligation"` agenda).
- **Structural blocking authority is state-resident:** `gate-readiness.ts` evaluators run in the Temporal workflow sandbox over `ChangeWorkflowState` only (`workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts` forbids `storage/`/`tools/`/`node:*`/agenda imports). Existing `checkRequiredObligationReleaseBlockers`/`checkRequiredObligationRouting` block on `requiredCritical` contract items + review-matrix rows.
- Agenda `category:"required-obligation"` items are **advisory routing**, NOT blocking authority. `AgendaItem` (`types/agenda.ts`) has no `change_id`/agent field — so agenda-prose matching must never gate anything (P33).
- Duplicate-report guard (`executeSubmit:640-658`) only blocks same-report-id resubmit; a new attempt re-runs consumers.

## Decision: blocking is state-resident (sandbox), promotion is advisory (agenda)

### Layer 1 — Schema (AC3 retained, + parity)
- Keep `DesignerDesignDimensionsSchema.superRefine` (notes required for `concern`/`n/a`).
- Add optional `required_follow_ups` to `DesignerSubagentReportSchema` (parity; lets designers emit explicit typed obligations that ride the existing critical→release rail).

### Layer 2 — Advisory promotion consumer (AC5 surfacing; advisory only)
- New `consumeDesignerDesignConcerns({ store, report, dryRun })`, invoked for `adv-designer` reports in `executeSubmit`.
- For each `design_dimensions.* === "concern"` and each `neighboring_recommendation`: create one `category:"required-obligation"` agenda item **with an attempt-stable dedupe key** `design-concern:<change_id>:<task_id>:<dimension|neighbor-index>` so re-submit at a higher attempt does not duplicate (idempotent create).
- Emits a typed `design_concern_promoted` consumer_warning per item.
- **Explicitly advisory** — surfacing/routing, never the gate authority.

### Layer 3 — Structural acceptance/release blocker (AC4, AC6) — state-resident, no new layer
- New sandbox-safe gate-readiness evaluator `checkUnresolvedDesignConcerns(state, gateId)` (reads `state.subagent_reports` + task reports only):
  - Computes, per task, the **latest** `adv-designer` report (max attempt).
  - A task has an *unresolved design concern* if its latest designer report has any `design_dimensions.* === "concern"` or non-empty `neighboring_recommendations` that are not dispositioned.
  - Blocks `acceptance` and `release` while any unresolved design concern exists.
- **Typed disposition path** (no prose, no accept-debt): a new `designConcernDispositionedSignal` records a typed disposition per `(task_id, dimension|neighbor-key)` with `disposition ∈ {fixed, rejected_with_evidence, split, fast_follow}` + evidence string. State holds `design_concern_dispositions[]`. The evaluator treats a concern as resolved when (a) a later designer report clears it, or (b) a matching typed disposition exists. `blocking` is the absence of disposition (i.e., still blocks).
- This reuses the existing gate-readiness blocker pattern (same shape as `checkRequiredObligationReleaseBlockers`), is replay-safe, and is fully machine-checkable.

### Layer 4 — Prep teeth retained (AC1)
- `prep-readiness.ts::checkFrontendApplicability` unchanged (structured metadata blocks prep; heuristics advisory).

### Layer 5 — Contract/spec/prose realignment (AC9, AC10)
- Contract already re-minted without accept-debt. Update specs/docs/commands/agents: strip residual accept-debt; point review/harden prose at the structural rail (the evaluator + disposition signal) rather than asking the reviewer LLM to remember.

### Layer 6 — Anti-slop tests (AC11)
- Label markdown keyword-presence asset tests **non-behavioral (asset presence only)**.
- Behavioral coverage: schema parse; consumer promotion + dedupe; evaluator blocks acceptance/release with an unresolved concern; disposition signal clears the block; later all-pass report clears the block; dryRun writes nothing.

## consumer_warning kind addition
- `SubagentConsumerWarningSchema` is `.strict()` enum → add `design_concern_promoted`, regenerate public schemas (`pnpm run schemas:generate`), update producers/tests.

## Out of scope
- No new lifecycle gate, no Storybook, no numeric scoring authority, no visual-regression baselines, no external tool vendoring.

## Validator resolution
- Rejected the original agenda-prose acceptance block (heuristic, P33 violation). Replaced with a state-resident sandbox evaluator + typed disposition signal.
- Agenda promotion demoted to advisory with attempt-stable dedupe.
- No agenda reads in `gate-readiness.ts` (boundary preserved); any tool-layer agenda use stays in `tools/`.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Delivered **structural** design-quality enforcement for ADV — concerns raised by `adv-designer` reports now block acceptance and release by code, not by reviewer prose. This change was re-defined from the top after an acceptance review found the first attempt was ~60% prompt text whose only tests asserted keyword presence.

## Outcome

- **Structural blocker (the teeth):** a sandbox-safe gate-readiness evaluator `checkUnresolvedDesignConcerns` reads persisted `adv-designer` reports from change state and emits a `DESIGN_CONCERN_UNRESOLVED` blocker on **acceptance and release** while a task's latest designer report has an undispositioned `design_dimensions` concern or `neighboring_recommendation`.
- **Typed disposition (no accepted_debt):** concerns clear only via a later all-pass report or a typed `designConcernDispositioned` signal (`adv_design_concern_disposition`: `fixed | rejected_with_evidence | split | fast_follow`, non-blank evidence). Dispositions persist through projection, re-seed, and continue-as-new.
- **Advisory promotion:** on report submit, each concern/neighbor is auto-promoted to a deduped `required-obligation` agenda item so nothing is silently lost — routing only, never the gate authority.
- **Kept real teeth:** designer dimension-notes `superRefine`; prep-readiness frontend-applicability validator (structured metadata owns routing; heuristics advisory).
- **Anti-slop:** keyword-presence asset tests are explicitly labeled non-behavioral and point at the behavioral test owners; contract re-minted with zero `accepted_debt` vocabulary.

## What changed vs the first attempt

The first attempt mapped designer evidence into the review matrix via reviewer prose with no consumer code — a concern could reach acceptance silently. This version makes the concern→block path code-owned and state-resident, satisfying SC2 ("design concerns visible before acceptance, not buried in sidecar reports") structurally.

## Verification

- Full suite (`bin/oc-test full`): **285 files / 4007 tests passed, 0 failures**.
- `pnpm run check` green; worker bundle builds; workflow-bundle boundary intact (evaluator is sandbox-safe).
- Behavioral coverage: `gate-readiness.test.ts` (evaluator, 7 cases), `subagent-report.test.ts` (consumer, 5 cases), `design-concern.test.ts` (tool, 5 cases), `change-state.test.ts` (disposition apply + persistence), `subagent-reports.test.ts` (schema, 6 cases).
- Independent acceptance review: **READY**; caught and fixed a disposition-persistence gap (projection/continue-as-new).
- Contract review matrix: 31/31 pass or respected, 0 failing.

## Remaining concerns

- `adv_change_validate strict` reports one non-blocking `NO_DELTAS` warning (spec edits are in-repo, not change deltas).
- Branch diverged from a base behind current trunk; several inherited stale frozen-snapshot tests were reconciled to trunk reality (spec versions, deploy ceiling, search-attribute lists, cli-surface row). Eventual PR merge should be routine but is wider than the design-quality code itself.

| C4 | constraint | Keep `adv-reviewer` as review/harden owner. | respected | adv-reviewer owns review/harden in adv-review.md/adv-harden.md/adv-reviewer.md. |

| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not treat subjective visual scoring as a replacement for typed evidence, review findings, and user acceptance. | respected | Categorical design_dimensions with rationale + typed disposition; no numeric aesthetic scoring introduced. |

| DONT5 | avoidance | Do not reassign review or harden ownership from `adv-reviewer` to `adv-designer`. | respected | Review/harden routed to adv-reviewer, not adv-designer. |
